memory.intro: Every agent gets its own long-lived memory: pinned preferences, recalled conversations and decisions, and hybrid keyword (BM25) + vector search. SQLite holds the real data; LadybugDB is a rebuildable vector + knowledge-graph index.
memory.denseNoEmbeddings: Embeddings unavailable — semantic search and the knowledge graph are off (keyword search only). Install the 'memory' extra (FlagEmbedding) on the backend.
memory.denseDown: Index unavailable — semantic search is degraded, falling back to keyword search.
memory.enableDesc: When off, nothing is captured, indexed, or searched. Changes apply to new sessions and agents (a backend restart may be needed).
memory.modeDesc: economical: no planner or reranker · balanced: used only when needed · deep: a few bounded follow-up searches.
memory.autoCaptureDesc: Notice when you say things like “from now on…” or “remember that…” and propose a pin.
memory.approvalPolicyDesc: manual: every item waits for your approval · auto_low_risk: clear, low-risk items are saved automatically (anything sensitive always needs approval).
memory.rerankerDesc: The BGE reranker only runs on ambiguous queries, high-risk actions, or in deep mode.
memory.curatorApiKeySet: Already set — type a new key to replace it, or leave blank to keep it.
memory.curatorApiKeyUnset: Not set. Use this together with a cheaper router base URL.
memory.recallMinSimHint: Dense hits below this are dropped → an off-topic turn recalls nothing. Scale depends on the embedding model (Qwen ≈ 0.34, bge-m3 ≈ 0.44).
memory.graphMaxHopsHint: How far to traverse the relation graph. 1 = precise for the star graph; 2 risks noise (1–3, default 1).
memory.rerankMinScoreHint: Candidates scoring below this after reranking are dropped (≥0, default 0.005).
memory.hubMaxDfHint: An entity mentioned by ≥ this fraction of memories (e.g. your own name) is a no-signal hub, excluded from graph expansion (0–1, default 0.5).

auth.title: Authentication
auth.desc: The master API key protects the entire API surface — it's what you use to log in and to call the API directly.
auth.rotateMsg: Every stored secret will be re-encrypted under the new key. This browser session seamlessly picks up the new bearer.
auth.rotateSuccess: Master key rotated. New bearer token is active.
auth.errMinLen: Key must be at least {n} characters.
auth.errSessionRefresh: Key rotated but session refresh failed — reload the page and log in with the new key.

system.title: System Settings
system.desc: Runtime toggles that apply without a full restart (unless marked).
system.timezoneHint: IANA timezone used by all time tools (e.g. Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh, America/New_York, Europe/London). Takes effect after backend restart.
system.sessionWindowHint: messages per agent session — needs backend restart to take effect.
system.errSessionRange: Session window must be between 10 and 10000.

data.title: Data Management
data.desc: Back up, restore, audit, and restart. Exports mask secrets by default — opt in only when you need a true backup.
data.exportHint: Download a JSON snapshot of every stored setting. Safe to share unless you include secrets.
data.importHint: Apply a previously exported JSON file. Merge (default) or replace per category.
data.replaceMode: Replace mode — delete keys missing from file
data.exportSecretsMsg: The file will contain plaintext API keys and tokens. Only do this if the file will be stored securely.
data.errInvalidFile: File is not a valid settings export (missing items).
data.replaceMsg: Replace mode DELETES any key in a category present in the file but missing from it.
data.mergeMsg: Merge mode only adds or updates keys from the file. Existing keys not in the file stay untouched.
data.restartRowHint: Requests a graceful shutdown. Only works when running under a process manager (docker-compose, systemd, PM2).
data.restartMsg: The process will exit and your container/process manager will bring it back up. API calls will fail for a few seconds.
data.restartSent: Restart signal sent. Backend is shutting down…
data.restartSentDropped: Restart signal sent (connection dropped as expected).

history.title: Change History
history.hint: Every setting change is logged. Use this to audit when a value was modified and by what action.
history.empty: No history entries match these filters.

llm.title: LLM Provider
llm.descPre: Each provider has its own credentials — switching tabs swaps the stored key and base URL independently. The
llm.descPost: provider (highlighted) is the one Jarvis boots with; other providers stay configured and can be referenced by fully-qualified model aliases (e.g.

openai.hint: Pick this for api.openai.com OR any OpenAI-wire proxy (9router, CLIProxyAPI, LiteLLM). Models are addressed as openai.{model}.

anthropic.hint: Official Anthropic API. Models are addressed as anthropic.{model}.

custom.label: Generic / Local
custom.hint: Self-hosted models addressed as generic.{model}. Remember to update default_model in fastagent.config.yaml if you want this slot to boot by default.

llm.baseUrlPlaceholder: Leave blank to use provider default
llm.baseUrlHint: Required when pointing at a proxy or local endpoint. Leave blank to use the provider default. Stored under
llm.keyKeepPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current key
llm.keyHintMid: before touching disk and synced to
llm.keyHintPost: so fast-agent subprocesses pick them up.
llm.clearKeyMsg: Remove the stored {label} API key? Jarvis will fail to reach {label} until you save a new one.
llm.restartMsg: Any in-progress chats will be interrupted. Your new LLM credentials take effect when the process comes back (~5–10s under docker-compose).
llm.restartRequested: Restart requested — backend will be back in a few seconds.
llm.restartSignalSent: Restart signal sent — backend going down now.
llm.restartBannerMsg: LLM credentials are cached inside fast-agent's in-memory clients at boot, so your changes won't reach the model until the backend process restarts.

services.encryptedEcho: Values are encrypted with the master key; the UI never echoes them back.

google.title: Google · Gmail + Calendar
google.desc: OAuth 2.0. Needs a Google Cloud project with Gmail + Calendar APIs enabled.

repo.title: Project Repository
repo.desc: Git URL agile-team agents (Dev, DSO, …) clone, push to, and file issues against. Stored as plain text for audit visibility.
repo.plainTextNote: Stored as plain text — a public git URL is not sensitive, and surfacing it here lets you confirm exactly which repo your agents will touch.
repo.encryptedNote: URL on file but stored encrypted from the setup wizard. Save again here to re-store as plain text.
repo.clearMsg: Agile-team agents will lose the project repo reference and refuse to clone/push until a new URL is set.

roborock.title: Roborock Vacuum
roborock.desc: IoT control through xiaomiy/python-miio. Uses the same email + password as the Roborock mobile app.
roborock.note: Credentials are encrypted at rest with the master key. 2FA codes are handled automatically through the Gmail integration.
roborock.disconnectMsg: Vacuum control tools will stop working until you re-enter your Roborock credentials.
roborock.errRequired: Both email and password are required.

github.title: GitHub (Dev Agent Git Access)
github.desc: Personal access token + git identity used by dev agents and the GitHub MCP. Bind-mounted into the container — never exposed via env.
github.bindNote: Token bind-mounted to the container as read-only; never echoed back.
github.disconnectMsg: Dev agents will stop being able to clone/push/pull private repos until a new token is configured.
github.errRequired: Token, git user name, and email are all required.

more.title: More services
more.desc: (Firebase, Home Assistant…) will land here over time. For now they still live in the Services tab of the Setup Wizard.

chat.title: Chat & Notification Voice
chat.descPre: Used by interactive chat replies and cron notifications. Default is Edge — free, no API key. Paid engines require a key (set inline below). Stories use a separate
chat.descPost: provider so paid engines never burn long-form quota.

stories.title: Stories Voice
stories.desc: Long-form audiobook narration. Always uses the free Edge engine — paid engines burn quota fast on book-length text. Only voice id and rate are editable.

stt.title: Speech recognition
stt.descPost: is Vietnamese-only but gives better accents on Vietnamese audio.
stt.wakeWordHint: Disabled by default — turn on to require "jarvis" before transcription kicks in.

turn.a1Pre: Only when you open Jarvis from outside the network the server lives on — phone on 4G/5G, office Wi-Fi, a friend's house. On
turn.a1Post: , the same LAN, or a VPN (Tailscale etc.) voice already works without it.
turn.step2Post: (free Cloudflare account is enough — the free tier includes 1 TB/month of relay traffic).
turn.step3Post: , give the TURN app any name (e.g.
turn.step4Post: below, then save both. Applies immediately — no restart. The key never leaves this server; browsers only ever receive short-lived credentials minted from it.

auth.passkeysDescPre: Sign in to the dashboard with Touch ID, Face ID, Windows Hello, or a security key — no password to remember. Passkeys are scoped to the current origin
auth.passkeysDescPost: : one registered on localhost cannot be used after you switch to a public domain.
auth.notSupported: Your browser does not support WebAuthn / passkeys. Sign in with the API key under Settings → General.
auth.addLabel: Add a passkey from this device
auth.apiKeyDescMid: remains the credential for non-browser callers (the Xiaozhi voice device, CLI tools, scripts) and is the recovery path if you lose every passkey: read it from
auth.apiKeyDescPost: , sign in once, then register a new passkey here.
auth.apiKeyRotate: Rotate it under Settings → General → Change API Key
auth.errSessionExpired: Your session has expired. Reload the page and sign in again.
auth.errLoadFailed: Failed to load passkeys. Check the backend.
auth.regUpdated: Existing passkey on this authenticator was updated.
auth.regSuccess: Passkey registered. You can now sign in with it.
auth.deleteConfirm: Delete passkey "{label}"? You will no longer be able to sign in with this authenticator on {rpId}.
auth.errAlreadyRegistered: This authenticator already has a passkey for this site.
auth.errUnsupported: Your browser does not support passkeys.
auth.errAuthFailed: Your session expired. Reload and sign in again.
auth.errNetwork: Network error. Check the backend is reachable.

compaction.intro: When an agent's context grows past the threshold, the backend snapshots the raw history (never deleted), summarizes the older middle section, and reloads the compacted context so the agent continues seamlessly. Inspect every compaction on the agent's Context Versions tab.
compaction.enableDesc: When off, contexts grow until the model's own limit — no snapshots or summaries are produced.
compaction.liveStatusDesc: Emit context_compaction_* events on the activity stream (toasts + live banner). Compaction itself is unaffected.
compaction.compactorModelDesc: Dedicated compactor agent's model (e.g. a large-window model so one call can summarize more history). Empty = use each agent's own model.
compaction.compactAtRatioHint: Compact when the context reaches this fraction of the limit. 0.3–0.95.
compaction.maxContextTokensHint: 0 = auto-detect from the model’s context window (recommended).
compaction.keepRecentHint: The newest N messages are never summarized. 2–100.
compaction.maxToolResultLabel: Max tool-result tokens kept in context
compaction.maxToolResultHint: Older oversized tool outputs are truncated beyond this (full text stays in the raw snapshot).
compaction.minSavingsLabel: Minimum savings to accept a compaction
compaction.minSavingsHint: Plans saving less than this fraction are rejected. 0–0.9.
compaction.compactorInputLabel: Compactor input budget (ratio of compactor window)
compaction.compactorInputHint: Fraction of the compactor model’s window one summary call may read. Larger = fewer calls (faster) but riskier. 0.1–0.9.
compaction.snapshotVersionsHint: Default number of compaction versions listed on the agent’s Context Versions tab.

experimental.bannerBody: These flags expose preview functionality that may change, regress, or be removed without notice. Enable only if you're comfortable with that.
experimental.approvalOff: ⚠ Approval off — at your own risk
experimental.selfImprovingDesc: Lets Jarvis author, edit, attach, and delete skills at runtime via the skill_server MCP tools. Toggle takes effect on the next tool call — no restart required. Every state change writes a notification so you can review what the agent did.
experimental.requireApprovalDesc: When ON (recommended), any schedule an AGENT creates that runs an agent turn is held for your approval before it can fire — your defence against a prompt-injected cron running unsupervised. Schedules YOU create on the dashboard are never gated. Turn this OFF to let agent-created schedules run immediately without approval — ⚠ use at your own risk.

runningTemplates.bannerStrong: Edits live in this team's DB only.
runningTemplates.bannerPre: Save here to take effect now; commit the same change to
runningTemplates.bannerPost: (YAML Config tab) to survive a recreate. Use "Reset role" to pull a yaml edit into this team.
runningTemplates.nothingToDiff: DB matches yaml — nothing to diff
runningTemplates.allowedFields: Allowed fields: instruction, servers, skills, server_overrides, model, role_display.
runningTemplates.noteAddedInDb: Role exists in DB only — not present in factory yaml. Saving the yaml would lose this role on next recreate.
runningTemplates.noteRemovedFromDb: Role exists in factory yaml only — removed from DB. Reset / reload would restore it.
runningTemplates.resetModalBodyPost: into this team's DB. Other roles are untouched and audited.
runningTemplates.resetModalNote: Already-running agents keep their stale in-memory template until the next restart — pick whether to force-respawn them now.
runningTemplates.resetReloadFailed: Synced {n} field(s) to DB, but force-reload failed — agents are still running the old template: {detail}
runningTemplates.resetSyncedReloaded: Reset · {n} field(s) synced from yaml · {respawned} agent(s) respawned.
runningTemplates.resetSyncedOnly: Reset · {n} field(s) synced from yaml. Running agents will pick up the change on next restart — click "Force reload" to apply now.
runningTemplates.discardMsg: You have unsaved edits to "{role}". Discard and switch?
runningTemplates.reloadMsg: SIGKILL every agent in this role mid-task and respawn with the current template. This is DESTRUCTIVE — in-progress work in those agents is lost. Continue?
runningTemplates.rollbackMsg: Writes a new audit row that reverts the field to its previous value. Intermediate edits to the same field will be silently overwritten.

yaml.editorPlaceholder: File is empty. Start typing YAML…
yaml.footerDirty: {n} line edited · ⌘S to save · backend hot-reloads
yaml.footerNotExist: File does not exist yet — save to create it.
yaml.discardMsg: You have {n} edited line(s) in the current file. Discard and switch?
yaml.savedTeamTemplateNote: Edits to factory yaml do NOT touch running teams — open Running Templates to apply.
yaml.revertMsg: Discard unsaved changes and revert to last saved content?

memory.degraded: Semantic search is degraded (vector index unavailable) — falling back to keyword search.

lane.graph: Knowledge-graph co-occurrence (MENTIONS) — surfaced via a shared entity

memory.scoreHint: rrf = raw lane-fusion rank score (keyword + vector). rerank = cross-encoder joint (query, memory) relevance — the authoritative order, near 1.0 when the query nearly restates the memory. conf = confidence the fact is true (0–1). ✓ = verified (user-confirmed or tool-verified).

approvalReason.unverified_evidence: Needs review even with auto-save on: the evidence for this memory couldn't be verified against your messages, so it wasn't auto-saved.
approvalReason.secret: Needs review: this looks like sensitive/secret content, which is never auto-saved.

chat.forceStopMessage: This will immediately SIGTERM every running subagent. Any side-effects already committed by them or by the orchestrator (database rows, written files, external API calls, sent messages) WILL NOT be rolled back automatically — you may need to clean them up by hand.
chat.stoppedDesc: Tools the agent already called (DB writes, files, sent messages) are not rolled back.
chat.forceStoppedDesc: {names}. Side-effects already committed are not rolled back.
chat.crawlSnag: Crawl hit a snag — Jarvis is on it…
chat.micAccessFailed: Mic access denied or voice session failed to start. Check Settings → Voice and browser permissions.
chat.dictate: Dictate — speak to fill the text box
chat.inputPlaceholder: Type a message, paste image (⌘V), or hit mic — Shift+⏎ for new line
chat.stopGenerationTitle: Stop generation. Tools already called are NOT undone. Shift-click to also force-terminate running subagents.
chat.emptySub: Send a message to interact with {name}
chat.deleteConversationConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{title}"? This action cannot be undone.
chat.bulkDeleteConfirm: Delete {n} conversation(s)? This action cannot be undone.

agents.title: Agents
agents.subtitle: Jarvis at the center, static workers and spawned teams orbiting outward.
agents.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, role, team, model…
agents.emptyDefaultHint: Spawn a team or open Settings to add agents

agentDetail.skillNotLoadedTitle: {name} was requested but did not load
agentDetail.builtinBannerStrong: This agent is defined in code (agent.py).
agentDetail.builtinBannerBody1: Attach/detach changes apply at runtime — they revert on backend restart unless you also edit
agentDetail.spawnedBannerBody: Skill changes here only affect the live instance — when the agent stops or the team is torn down, all attachments are lost. Edit the team template to make changes persistent across spawns.
agentDetail.skillsSharedHint: Skills are shared across agents. Editing one updates every agent that references it.
agentDetail.allSkillsAttached: All available skills are already attached.
agentDetail.waitingSkills: Waiting for agent runtime to report which skills it actually loaded…
agentDetail.builtinSkillTitle: Ships with Jarvis. Editable, but cannot be deleted.
agentDetail.skillFailedTitle: The agent requested this skill but it failed to load (missing file, parse error, or wrong skills dir). It is NOT injected into the agent's prompt.
agentDetail.noSkillsAttached: No skills attached to this agent.
agentDetail.waitingServers: Waiting for agent runtime to report which MCP servers actually attached…
agentDetail.serverFailedTitle: MCP server failed to attach. The agent does NOT see these tools.
agentDetail.serverNotAttached: MCP server is not attached. No error message reported.
agentDetail.noSnapshots: No context window snapshots recorded yet
agentDetail.noCompactions: No context compactions yet — versions appear here when the agent's context is automatically compacted (see Settings → Context Compaction)
agentDetail.noActivity: No activity recorded for this agent
agentDetail.attachBuiltinMsg: {name} is defined in code (agent.py). Attaching '{skill}' will only apply at runtime and revert when the backend restarts unless you also add it to {name}'s get_skills(...) call.
agentDetail.attachSpawnedMsg: {name} is a spawned agent. Attaching '{skill}' only affects the live instance — it will be lost when the agent stops. Edit the team template to persist this change across spawns.
agentDetail.attachedRuntime: Attached '{skill}' — runtime only, reverts on restart.
agentDetail.detachCardMsg: '{skill}' will be removed from {name}. The skill itself stays in the library.
agentDetail.detachSpawnedMsg: {name} is a spawned agent. Detaching only affects the live instance; '{skill}' will reattach on the next spawn unless you also edit the team template.
agentDetail.detachCodeMsg: {name} is code-based. Detaching only applies at runtime; the skill will reattach on next backend restart unless you remove it from agent.py.

tree.staticCannotDelete: Static agents (built-in or default) cannot be deleted

skillEditor.nameRuleError: Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only (no leading/trailing hyphen).
skillEditor.discardMessage: You have unsaved changes. Discard them and close?
skillEditor.builtinTitle: Ships with Jarvis. Editable, but cannot be deleted.
skillEditor.usedByNote: Changes apply to all of them.
skillEditor.nameHint: Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. Becomes the skill's directory name.
skillEditor.emptyPreview: Empty preview — start typing to see rendered markdown.
skillEditor.deleteReferencedBy: It is currently referenced by {n} agent(s):
skillEditor.deleteRefsCleanup: Those references will be cleaned up automatically.

cmdk.placeholder: Go to agent, page, or setting…

authGate.reasonKeyRotated: Master key was rotated. Paste the new key to continue.
authGate.reasonExpired: Your session has expired. Please log in again.
authGate.reasonMaxLifetime: Session reached its maximum lifetime. Please log in again.
authGate.reasonInvalidCredentials: Wrong API key. Try again, or reset via setup.
authGate.errCredentialUnknown: This passkey is not recognised on this deployment. Sign in with your API key, then re-register the passkey.
authGate.errRateLimited: Too many attempts. Wait a minute and try again.
authGate.errUnsupported: Your browser does not support passkeys.
authGate.errNetwork: Network error. Check the backend is reachable.
authGate.errNotConfigured: Backend not configured. Run setup first.
authGate.title: Authentication required
authGate.neutralHint: Paste your JARVIS_API_KEY to access the dashboard.
authGate.forgotKey: Forgot your key? Re-run Setup Wizard →
authGate.fineprint: KEY STORED IN HTTP-ONLY COOKIE · NO LOCAL TOKEN
authGate.tagline: Just A Rather Very Intelligent System

googleOauth.errBothRequired: Both client_id and client_secret are required.
googleOauth.errPopupBlocked: Popup blocked — please allow popups for this site.
googleOauth.errPasteUrl: Paste the URL from your browser address bar.
googleOauth.errUrlMissing: URL missing code or state — make sure you copied the full redirected URL.
googleOauth.errStateMismatch: State mismatch — restart the flow and paste the URL from this session.
googleOauth.disconnectConfirmMessage: Gmail and Calendar tools will stop working until you reconnect.
googleOauth.resetConfirmMessage: Your saved Client ID & Secret (and any connected tokens) will be removed. You will need to paste new credentials and re-connect.
googleOauth.expired: Expired — will refresh on next use.
googleOauth.heading: Google (Gmail + Calendar)
googleOauth.intro: Connect once. Your agents will use the stored refresh token — no browser pop-up needed for subsequent calls.
googleOauth.pasteCredsPre: Paste Google OAuth credentials from the
googleOauth.pasteCredsPost: Pick the client type that matches what you created there.
googleOauth.desktopAppHint: No redirect URI to configure — easiest for a self-hosted Jarvis.
googleOauth.webAppHintPost: to the client's authorised redirect URIs.
googleOauth.step1Post: A new tab opens on Google.
googleOauth.step2Pre: Pick your account and allow access. Google sends you to a page that shows
googleOauth.step3Pre: Copy that full URL, paste below, then press
googleOauth.webClientHint: Using your Web-application OAuth client. Click "Connect Google" to open the consent popup.
googleOauth.noRefreshToken: No refresh token on file — you'll need to reconnect when the access token expires.
googleOauth.enableApisHeading: Enable these APIs in Google Cloud Console
googleOauth.enableApisHint: One-time per project. OAuth consent alone is not enough — Gmail/Calendar calls fail with 403 until the API is enabled.
googleOauth.noProjectNumber: Couldn't parse a project number from your client_id — links open without a pre-selected project, so pick the right one on the Console page.

floatingChat.empty: No messages yet. Type below to chat with {name} — replies stream here without leaving this view.

terminal.pausing: Pausing… (waiting for current step to finish)

bulkInject.textareaPlaceholder: Type a prompt · paste an image (⌘V) · attach a file · press the mic — broadcasts to all selected

flowLegend.tip: edge thickness = token flow · brighter = active

meetings.title: Meeting Room
meetings.noMeetingsHint: Agents create them via the create_meeting tool.
meetings.noMeetingsTabHint: Meetings appear here when agents create them
meetings.noDescriptionPrefix: No description was provided. Tip: pass
meetings.noDescriptionSuffix: for long-form context (links, scope, deliverables) — keeps agenda short for the dashboard title.
meetings.selectMeetingBodyPrefix: Pick a meeting from the list to view its transcript in real time. When agents call
meetings.selectMeetingBodySuffix: , meetings appear in the left rail and auto-stream once a participant joins.
meetings.selectMeetingChoose: Choose a meeting from the list to view its transcript in real time

scheduler.desc: Cron jobs and reminders. Catch failures early; intervene with quick actions.
scheduler.noJobs: No cron jobs yet. Create one to get started.
scheduler.approvalPendingTitle: Created by an agent — awaiting your approval before it can run
scheduler.approvalRejectedTitle: Approval rejected — this job will not run
scheduler.cronHintPrefix: minute hour day month weekday — e.g.
scheduler.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete job '{name}'? This cannot be undone.

approvals.descPrefix: Each item is a plan or doc an agent sent via
approvals.descSuffix: Comment by line or selection range before approving.
approvals.hintText: Click line numbers to comment · select text to comment a range
approvals.commentsSentNote: Comments are sent with resolve · agents will see them
approvals.resolvePlaceholder: Optional comment for the agent (sent with approve/reject)…
approvals.catalogPrefix: No pending approvals. When an agent calls
approvals.catalogSuffix: it will appear here and the agent will block until you resolve.
approvals.historyHintSuffix: in the queue tabs to browse past decisions.
approvals.pickFromQueue: Pick an item from the queue to review.
approvals.descTeamPlan: PM submits sprint plans — backlog, role assignments, DoD.
approvals.descArchitecture: SA submits ADRs / system design before DEV implements.
approvals.descImplPlan: DEV submits an impl plan (file map, branch, scope) before writing.
approvals.descBrd: BA submits a Business Requirements Doc for acceptance review.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.emptySub: They appear when scheduler jobs complete.

tokens.descTail: SSE events. Mono-numeric breakdown by agent / model / tool.
tokens.noTokenData: No token data for this period
tokens.noAgentUsage: No agent usage for this period.
tokens.noModelUsage: No model usage for this period.
tokens.noToolBreakdown: No per-tool breakdown available for this period.

comingSoon.bodyRest: dashboard is in development and will arrive in a future update.

oauth.title: Google OAuth
oauth.canClose: You can close this window now.
oauth.missingCodeState: Missing code/state in redirect. Please restart the flow.
oauth.noOpener: No opener window. Close this tab and restart the flow from Settings → Services.

mcp.descRest: on first boot — editable but not deletable.
mcp.searchPlaceholder: Filter by name, command, or url…
mcp.toolsEmptyTail: to fetch from a live connection)
mcp.codeAgentRevert: Code-based agent — change reverts on restart
mcp.eventsHint: Live + history, newest first. Filtered to this server.
mcp.selectOrCreate: Select a server on the left, or create a new one.
mcp.deleteMsgAttached: Will detach from {n} agent(s) and delete the server. Any in-flight tool calls will be interrupted.
mcp.deleteMsgCatalog: Server will be removed from the catalog.

skillsLib.descRest: Editing here updates every agent that references the skill. Built-ins are editable but cannot be deleted.
skillsLib.noMatch: No skills match the current filter.
skillsLib.codeAgentRevert: Code-based agent — change reverts on restart
skillsLib.attachConfirmMsg: {agent} is defined in code (agent.py), so this attachment is runtime-only — it will revert on backend restart unless you also add '{skill}' to {agent}'s get_skills(...) call.
skillsLib.toastAttachedRuntime: Attached '{skill}' to {agent} — runtime only, will revert on restart.
skillsLib.detachConfirmMsg: '{skill}' will be removed from {agent}. The skill itself stays in the library.

teamMonitor.title: Team Monitor
teamMonitor.subtitle: Real-time multi-agent activity stream
teamMonitor.builtinProtected: Built-in agent — protected from bulk delete
teamMonitor.noActiveAgents: No active agents at the moment
teamMonitor.pausedByApproval: Agent is paused by pending approval {id}. Resolve the approval first.
teamMonitor.tooltipOnlyBuiltin: Only built-in agents are selected — built-ins cannot be deleted. Tick some user agents first.
teamMonitor.tooltipSelectFirst: Select agents from the dropdown first
teamMonitor.removeAgentLead: Are you sure you want to remove
teamMonitor.removeAgentTail: ? This will delete its card file, registry entries, and activity history.
teamMonitor.bulkDeleteBody: The following agents and their conversation history will be permanently removed:

auth.subtitleConfirm: A master key is already configured on this server (likely from .env or a previous setup). Re-enter it below to confirm and continue.
auth.subtitleWelcome: First, protect your instance with an API key. You'll use it to log in and to call the API directly.
auth.existingBody1: Paste the key you already have (from your
auth.existingBody2: or deployment config) and click Continue — we'll confirm it matches and move on.
auth.existingBody3: If you've lost the existing key, reset the server with a new value in
auth.placeholderExisting: Paste the key from your .env / cheatsheet
auth.saveBody: This is the API key you'll use to log in to Jarvis and to call the API directly. Store it somewhere safe — if you lose it you'll need to set a new one in .env and reconfigure access.
auth.errMinLen: API key must be at least {n} characters.

llm.title: LLM provider configuration
llm.subtitle: Configure the AI model provider that powers Jarvis. Use OpenAI, Anthropic, or any OpenAI-compatible API.
llm.openaiExamples: gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o1-mini — or whatever alias your proxy exposes
llm.openaiHint: Use this for api.openai.com OR any OpenAI-compatible proxy (9router, CLIProxyAPI, LiteLLM — they speak OpenAI wire format). Models are addressed as openai.{model}.
llm.anthropicHint: Official Anthropic API. Models will be addressed as anthropic.{model}.
llm.customHint: For self-hosted models behind OpenAI-compatible endpoints. Models will be addressed as generic.{model} — remember to update default_model in fastagent.config.yaml.
llm.alreadyStored: · already stored, leave blank to keep

services.title: External services
services.subtitle: Connect optional services to extend Jarvis. You can revisit these from Settings → Services later.
services.roborockDesc: Control Roborock vacuums via the Roborock cloud. Uses your Roborock account email + password (same credentials as the Roborock mobile app).
services.turnDesc: Lets voice chat work when you open Jarvis from OUTSIDE your home network — phone on 4G/5G, office Wi-Fi. Not needed for localhost / LAN / VPN use, and you can add it later in Settings → Voice. Get both values free at dash.cloudflare.com → Realtime → TURN → Create (free tier: 1 TB/month relay traffic).
services.githubDesc: Personal access token so dev agents can git clone/push/pull private repos (and drive the GitHub MCP). Name + email are used as the commit identity. Create a token at https://github.com/settings/tokens with the "repo" scope.
services.repoDescStrong: Jarvis uses this to evolve itself.
services.repoDescBody1: Agile-team agents (Dev, DSO, …) inject this URL into their system prompt — so when you tell them
services.repoDescBody2: , this is the repo they clone, push commits to, and file issues against. Without it, self-improvement prompts have nowhere to land.

yaml.title: Advanced configuration (optional)
yaml.subtitle: Review and customize your YAML config files. Defaults work for most setups — you can skip this step.
yaml.calloutBody1: for non-default MCP servers / provider overrides. Saves are validated with
yaml.editorPlaceholder: File is empty. Start typing YAML…
yaml.fileNotExist: File does not exist yet — save to create it.
yaml.discardMessage: You have unsaved changes in the current file. Discard them and switch?
yaml.revertMessage: Discard unsaved changes and revert to last saved content?
yaml.continueMessage: You have unsaved YAML edits. Continue without saving them?
yaml.leaveMessage: You have unsaved YAML edits. Leave without saving?

verify.subtitleComplete: Jarvis is configured and ready to go. Click Launch when you're ready.
verify.subtitleReady: Here's the final summary. Review and launch when ready.
verify.masterKeyMissing: Required — please go back to Step 1
verify.llmMissing: Required — please go back to Step 2
verify.launchAnyway: Launch anyway (I'll configure later in Settings)
verify.fineprint: You can always change these settings later from the Settings page.

stories.emptySub: Ask the AI to crawl stories from the web to fill your library.
stories.selectStoryHint: Pick a story from the library to view its chapter list.
stories.deleteConfirm:
Are you sure you want to delete the story "{title}"?
The audio cache will also be removed.
